mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 50 percent of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5 percentage point threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term positive equity momentum over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances during expansionary periods, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative equity returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the signal fails to predict direction during periods of stable growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP contraction and rising unemployment confirm a synchronized economic downturn, which severely impairs corporate profitability and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in housing starts indicate strong construction sector demand and rising household wealth effects, which generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during low-rate environments.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in more than 55 percent of instances when the federal funds rate is below 1.0 percent, or the predictive power vanishes in high-rate regimes.
